body:
## What & why · ماذا ولماذا

Turns the existing machinery into **one canonical, verified run path** that
stands the company up day-to-day. The repo already had ICP scoring,
qualification, draft generation, and a full bilingual website — but no single
command tied them into a daily operator workflow, and there was no
out-of-the-box seed so a fresh clone could actually produce output.

`make day0` now generates a dated **Operator Pack** under `data/day0/<date>/`:

| File | What it is |
|---|---|
| `OPERATOR_PACK.md` | Index — work it top to bottom |
| `prospects.md` / `.csv` | ICP-scored, ranked shortlist + recommended offer (5-rung ladder) |
| `call_list.md` | Today's prioritized contacts + bilingual talking points |
| `draft_pack.md` | Pre-screened outreach drafts, **queued for founder approval** |
| `scorecard.md` | Founder daily scorecard |

**The system sends nothing.** It scores, ranks, drafts, and queues — the
founder approves and contacts. This is doctrine rung **#8** (no external action
without approval), enforced by tests. النظام لا يرسل شيئاً — المؤسس يوافق ويتواصل.

## Changes

- **`scripts/dealix_day0.py`** — canonical Day-0 orchestrator, built on the
  existing `sales_os` ICP + qualification engines (no new scoring philosophy).
- **`data/prospects.seed.csv`** — clearly-labelled **SAMPLE** seed (reserved
  `*.example.sa` domains) so `make day0` runs on a clean clone. The founder's
  real list goes in `data/prospects.csv` (gitignored → PII stays out of git).
- **`Makefile`** — `make day0` + `make site-build` canonical targets.
- **`tests/test_dealix_day0.py`** — enforce determinism + doctrine: bilingual
  disclaimer, `governance_decision`, no guaranteed-outcome language, no
  auto-send wording, and the doctrine-violation reject path.
- **`tests/test_no_linkedin_scraper_string_anywhere.py`** — allowlist a
  documentation file that names the guard test (**pre-existing CI failure**,
  unrelated to this feature).
- **`docs/launch/DAY0_REAL_ACTIVATION.md`** + **README** — honest "real today vs
  needs your input" launch guide.

## Verification

- `make day0` → 16 prospects scored, Operator Pack written ✅
- `cd frontend && npm run build` → all routes build clean ✅
- `pytest tests/test_no_*.py tests/test_dealix_day0.py tests/test_commercial_doctrine.py` → **20 passing** ✅ (was 1 failing before this PR)

## Honest scope · النطاق بصدق

This makes the **pipeline** real and runnable. To go fully live the founder
still supplies: a real prospect list, LLM API key, Moyasar live cutover, email
OAuth, and does the actual contacting (talking points are ready — the call is a
human, approval-gated step by doctrine). Details in
`docs/launch/DAY0_REAL_ACTIVATION.md`.

---
_Estimated value is not Verified value / القيمة التقديرية ليست قيمة مُتحقَّقة._

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FmGNLJ3D76gpK75CBbYT1d

commits.messageBody:
…y up

- scripts/dealix_day0.py: canonical Day-0 orchestrator. Loads the prospect
  CSV the founder controls, scores each via sales_os ICP + qualification,
  ranks them, and writes a dated Operator Pack (prospects, call list with
  bilingual talking points, pre-screened outreach drafts, daily scorecard).
  Sends nothing — founder approves and contacts (doctrine #8).
- data/prospects.seed.csv: clearly-labelled SAMPLE seed so `make day0` runs
  out-of-the-box; real list goes in data/prospects.csv (gitignored, PII-safe).
- Makefile: `make day0` + `make site-build` canonical run paths.
- tests/test_dealix_day0.py: enforce determinism + doctrine (bilingual
  disclaimer, governance_decision, no guarantees, no auto-send, reject path).
- fix(tests): allowlist docs/enterprise_architecture/TESTS_REQUIRED.md in the
  linkedin_scraper string guard (pre-existing CI failure).
- docs/launch/DAY0_REAL_ACTIVATION.md + README: honest "real now vs needs your
  input" launch guide.

commits.messageBody:
Format scripts/dealix_day0.py and tests/test_dealix_day0.py to pass the
repo lint gate (ruff select E/W/F/I/B/C4/UP/N/S/SIM/RUF + black --check).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FmGNLJ3D76gpK75CBbYT1d
commits.messageHeadline: style(day0): ruff + black clean — noqa E402 on path-deferred imports

commits.messageBody:
CI infra (pre-existing failures on every PR; founder-approved to fix here):
- Gitleaks: replace license-gated gitleaks-action@v2 with the free gitleaks
  CLI (pinned 8.18.4) using the existing .gitleaks.toml.
- Trivy: bump aquasecurity/trivy-action 0.24.0 (deleted tag) -> v0.28.0.
- .gitleaks.toml: allowlist test fixtures (tests/*.py) and the two
  secret-scanning verify scripts whose own grep patterns trip the rules.
  Verified locally with gitleaks 8.18.4: 16 false positives -> 0 leaks.

CodeQL on scripts/dealix_day0.py:
- Wrap intentional multi-line strings in parens (3x implicit
  string-concatenation-in-list warnings).
- Extract _warm_list_drafts helper to drop the unused-variable finding.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FmGNLJ3D76gpK75CBbYT1d
commits.messageHeadline: fix(ci+codeql): unblock security scans + resolve CodeQL findings

commits.messageBody:
…nary

Trivy (now actually running) flagged 20 CRITICAL/HIGH fixable CVEs, all from
next@15.1.3 in frontend/ and apps/web/ — including CVE-2025-55182 (critical
pre-auth RCE). Upgrade next (+ eslint-config-next) to 15.5.19, the latest 15.x
patch that clears all 20 without a major-version jump.

- frontend + apps/web: next -> 15.5.19, lockfiles regenerated, next-env.d.ts
  refreshed (typed-routes reference). Both `next build` and `tsc --noEmit`
  pass locally; Trivy fs on both lockfiles: 0 CRITICAL/HIGH.
- repository-hardening.yml: run Trivy via the official pinned binary (0.70.0)
  instead of trivy-action — the action pulled a flagged supply-chain advisory
  (GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23, surfaced by Dependency Review) and a broken internal
  setup-trivy@v0.2.1 reference. Scoped to --scanners vuln so it doesn't
  duplicate the Gitleaks secret scan.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FmGNLJ3D76gpK75CBbYT1d
